fact: November 26 sits exactly 35 days before New Year's Eve - that's exactly 5 weeks!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many trees have completely given up on their leaves and stand totally naked against the sky.
Squirrels are in full panic mode right now, burying nuts like crazy because their tiny brains know winter is almost here.
The sun sets before 5 PM in most of the northern United States on this date - dinner time happens in complete darkness!
November 26 falls during Sagittarius season, when people born are supposed to be adventure-loving and can't sit still.
In Antarctica, penguin chicks are hatching right now because it's actually spring down there - the opposite of everywhere else!
This date marks when many northern lakes start forming their first thin sheets of ice that crack and pop like Rice Krispies.
Deer are typically in their mating season around now, which means male deer are acting crazy and fighting each other with their antlers.
The moon looks extra bright and crisp on cold November nights because there's less water vapor in the air to blur it.
November 26 is exactly one month before December 26 - it's like the calendar's way of giving you a sneak peek of what's coming.
In northern Canada, the aurora borealis (northern lights) are often at their most active because the long nights give them more time to dance across the sky.
Bears in cold regions are getting super sleepy right now and some are already snoring away in their winter dens.
